codeReview.summary: Clean, consistent implementation of Codex wire method adapters for DroidAcpTransport::send_request(). All 6 VAL-DROIDACP-MAP assertions are covered by 19 tests (plus 3 regression tests). The implementation follows the established patterns from sibling transports (PiAcpTransport, DroidNativeTransport) and correctly maps Droid stream-json semantics. TDD approach confirmed via transcript — tests written first, failed (red), then implementation added (green).

issues.description: Test `droid_acp_codex_turn_interrupt_no_output_to_transport` has stale comments from development ('Need to re-init after reset...', 'Actually, reset() clears...'). These are harmless but add noise. The test logic itself is correct — reset() clears written buffer, then interrupt is sent and output is verified empty.

summary: PASS — DroidAcpTransport Codex wire method mapping is well-implemented with full test coverage. The implementation correctly handles: thread/start (prompt + synthetic ThreadStart with session ID from system/init or generated UUID), turn/start (prompt only, no re-init), turn/interrupt (Ok(Null), no transport write), thread/list (current session or empty), no-op methods (archive/rollback/name/set), and collaborationMode/list (empty). 19 new tests cover all 6 validation assertions plus regression. All 992 tests pass, clippy clean. One minor non-blocking issue: stale development comments in one test.
